title: decachaeta ovandensis
intro: Decachaeta ovandensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the 'Mexican daisy'. It has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive issues. Decachaeta ovandensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms of traditional medicine.
